title: -Contract: Bauman Landscape and Construction, Inc. for Adeline Street Landscaping Improvements Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager during recess:
1. Approving the plans and specifications for the Adeline Street Landscaping
Improvements Project; and
2. Accepting the bid of the lowest responsive and responsible bidder, Bauman
Landscape and Construction Inc.; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments,
extensions or other change orders until completion of the project in accordance
with the approved plans and specifications, with Bauman Landscape and
Construction Inc. for the Adeline Street Landscaping Improvements Project, in an
amount not to exceed $357,000 which includes a contract amount of $297,500,
and a 20% contingency in the amount of $59,500.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
The Adeline Street Landscaping Improvements project is partially funded by Measure
T1 which is approaching a spending deadline. If action is delayed until after Council
recess, the construction project would be delayed. This would in turn delay the
expenditure of Measure T1 funds. Awarding this contract as soon as possible will
increase the City’s ability to complete the work by the spending deadline.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for this contract is available in the FY 2026 budget in the Measure T1 Fund
(Fund 511) and Parks Tax Fund (Fund 138). No other funding is required, and no other
projects will be delayed due to this expenditure.

sections.current_situation:
This improvement project is located along the eastern edge of Adeline Street at Martin
Luther King Jr. Way near the Berkeley/Oakland City limit. It is located along one of the
busiest corridors and entry points to the City from the south. This high-profile site is in
need of improvement and beautification.
The project will include planting of perennials and flowering shrubs, tree removal,
installation of new irrigation, fencing, decorative boulders, and ancillary work in
accordance with the Project plans and specifications.
The contract will include a 20% contingency of the base bid to cover related unexpected
or miscellaneous construction services.
sections.background:
The project was advertised for bids on Wednesday, November 12, 2025, and bids were
opened on Tuesday, December 16, 2025. The City received 11 bids, from an apparent
low base bid of $297,500 to an apparent high base bid of $501,847. The determination
of the lowest responsive and responsible bidder was based on the price for base bid
work, as indicated in the bid documents.
Bauman Landscape and Construction Inc. was the lowest responsive and responsible
bidder. Staff conducted references checks and received satisfactory feedback. Staff
recommends that a contract for this project be awarded to Bauman Landscape and
Construction Inc.
The Living Wage Ordinance does not apply to this project since construction contracts
are, pursuant to City policy, subject to State prevailing wage laws. The contractor will
need to submit a certification of Compliance with Equal Benefits Ordinance. The
contract will be subject to the First Source Construction Agreement.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The construction contract includes requirements to comply with the City’s
Environmentally Preferable Purchasing Policy and Construction and Demolition Waste

sections.rationale:
The Adeline Street Landscaping Improvements Project is needed as part of the City’s
ongoing program to repair, renovate, improve, and beautify aging park facilities and
City-owned green spaces. The City does not have in-house labor or equipment
resources to complete this improvement project.
sections.alternatives:
None
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks Recreation & Waterfront, 510-981-6700
Evelyn Chan, Supervising Civil Engineer, Parks Recreation & Waterfront, 510-981-6430

Page 3
Page 4 of 5
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: BAUMAN LANDSCAPE AND CONSTRUCTION INC. FOR ADELINE
STREET LANDSCAPING IMPROVEMENTS PROJECT
WHEREAS, the Adeline Streetscape is in need of improvement and beautification; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the in-house labor nor the equipment necessary to
undertake this project; and
WHEREAS, invitation for bids was duly advertised on November 12, 2025, bids were
opened on December 16, 2025, and the City received 11 bids; and
WHEREAS, Bauman Landscape and Construction was determined to be the lowest
responsive and responsible bidder, and references for Bauman Landscape and
Construction were provided and checked out satisfactorily; and
WHEREAS, funds are available in the FY 2026 budget in the Measure T1 Phase 2 Fund
(Fund 511), and Parks Tax (Fund 138).
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager during recess approving the Plans
and Specifications for Specification No. 26-11768-C for the Adeline Street Landscaping
Improvements Project are approved; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the construction contract is awarded to Bauman
Landscape and Construction as the lowest responsive and responsible bidder; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the City Manager is authorized to execute a contract
and any amendments, extensions, or change orders until completion of the project in
accordance with the approved plans and specifications with Bauman Landscape and
Construction Inc. for the Adeline Street Landscaping Improvements Project in an
amount not to exceed $357,000, which includes a contract amount of $297,500 and a
20% contingency in the amount of $59,500 for unforeseen circumstances. A record
signature copy of the agreement and any amendments to be on file in the Office of the
City Clerk.
Page 5 of 5
Attachment 2: Bid Results
